mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls or stays near the effective lower bound, discount-rate pressure eases and equity duration benefits dominate, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts occur but forward returns do not improve relative to neutral rate periods.
failure_condition: Triggered 20-day forward returns are not higher than unconditional returns, or the sign is negative in more than 55% of easing observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks slowing household income growth and weaker risk appetite, which should push the S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days. This claim fails if unemployment spikes are followed by positive equity returns rather than de-risking.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds the 3-month change threshold, SPX still posts positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS indicates firmer current demand and better near-term revenue momentum for large-cap firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should disappear if sales growth slows or reverses.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03 or positive-sales-growth periods do not outperform flat/negative periods on 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST remains depressed, production is outpacing new housing supply and the industrial side of the economy is strengthening, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The signal fails if industrial strength is not accompanied by better equity performance.
failure_condition: Joint high-INDPRO/low-HOUST states do not outperform other states on 60-day returns, or the interaction term is insignificant across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in HOUST after a prior slump signals improving residential demand and credit transmission, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim is falsified if housing starts recover without any equity follow-through.
failure_condition: After the housing-starts threshold is crossed, 60-day SPX returns are not positive more often than negative over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling FEDFUNDS and stable CPIAUCSL improves real-rate conditions, lifts valuation multiples, and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts are accompanied by rising inflation expectations that offset the discount-rate effect.
failure_condition: Short-horizon returns are not positive after simultaneous rate declines and stable CPI, or the interaction has zero predictive value in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, a continued decline in UNRATE paired with payroll gains in PAYEMS confirms labor-market healing and should support positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if payroll improvements do not translate into equity strength.
failure_condition: When unemployment falls and payrolls rise together, 60-day forward returns are not above baseline or the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: If GDPC1 is expanding while RSAFS weakens, aggregate output is being supported by non-retail components and equity gains should be muted or negative because final demand is not broad-based. The hypothesis is confirmed by underperformance in mixed-growth conditions and rejected if such mixed signals still lead to strong rallies.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth positive while RSAFS 3-month growth is negative
failure_condition: Mixed GDP-up / retail-sales-down states do not underperform over 20 days, or the sign of the forward return is positive in most such instances.
